title: Senior Quality Assurance Engineer
description:
**Senior Quality Assurance Engineer**


We are looking for an experienced Quality Assurance Engineer/Quality Manager to lead and coordinate software quality activities across multiple programs. In this role, you will define, drive, and follow up on quality actions required to close identified gaps in the product, working closely with program and project managers as well as external stakeholders. You will be part of a team responsible for software release, integration, and testing within a core system environment in the automotive domain.

**Responsibilities**

* Lead and coordinate software quality activities across multiple programs
* Define, drive, and follow up on quality actions to close identified product gaps
* Collaborate with program and project managers as well as external stakeholders on all quality-related matters
* Contribute to shaping and maintaining processes within the process framework
* Ensure compliance with relevant automotive ISO standards, including ISO/IEC 15504 and ISO 12207
* Support software release, integration, and testing activities within the team

**Requirements**

* M.Sc. in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or equivalent
* Several years of experience in software quality assurance across different types of systems and solutions
* Strong knowledge of complex systems including embedded SW/HW in the automotive domain
* Deep knowledge of relevant automotive quality standards, including ISO/IEC 15504 and ISO 12207
* Hands-on experience in software development
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills
* Highly proficient in English, both written and spoken
* Self-motivated, analytical, and able to drive initiatives independently

**Meriting**

* Experience working within automotive R&D environments
* Familiarity with SWEP or equivalent software engineering process frameworks
* Additional language skills relevant to the working environment

**Start:** 2026-04

**Duration:** Until 2026-12 (extension possible)

**Location:** Gothenburg, Sweden (on-site, 0 days remote)

**About Rasulson Consulting** Rasulson Consulting is a specialized staffing and recruitment firm within Test and QA. We partner with established technology companies as well as innovative startups to offer attractive career opportunities for professionals looking to grow within testing, quality assurance, and QA engineering. With our strong technical expertise and extensive network, we effectively match the right consultant with the right assignment. At Rasulson Consulting, you will receive personalized guidance, ongoing feedback, and the opportunity to take the next step in your career.

title: Quality Assurance Engineer/Quality Manager
description:
**We are looking for a Quality Assurance Engineer/Quality Manager for a global company in Gothenburg. Start is in April, 9-month limited contract to begin with.**


As a Quality Assurance Engineer/Quality Manager, you will lead and coordinate software quality activities across multiple programs. Your primary responsibility is to define, drive, and follow up on the quality actions required to close identified gaps in our product.


In this role, you will work closely with program and project managers within SWEP as well as external stakeholders to ensure alignment on all quality related matters. You will be part of a team of project leaders responsible for software release, integration, and testing for our core system.


You will also contribute to shaping and maintaining the processes within our process framework to ensure compliance with relevant automotive ISO standards. Adherence to ISO/IEC 15504.
  

  

Success in this position requires strong collaboration skills and the ability to work effectively with colleagues within the team, across SWEP, and throughout R&D.

**Do you fit the profile?**


We believe that you are a highly skilled Quality Assurance Engineer (not a tester) or Manager with several years of experience in software quality assurance for different types of systems and solutions. You are a genuine problem solver with a positive and solution-oriented mind-set. You are known to be a team player, you are curious and a quick learner.

**Competence requirements and required experience:**

* Good knowledge of complex systems including embedded SW/HW in the automotive domain
* Good knowledge of software quality engineering practices and standards
* Deep knowledge of the relevant automotive quality standards e.g. (ISO/IEC 15504, ISO 12207)
* Hands-on experience in software development
* M.Sc. in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering or equivalent
* Very good in English, both written and spoken
* Flexible, enthusiastic, methodical, analytical, and with high professional integrity
* Self-motivated and able to drive initiatives
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills


This is a full-time consultant position in Gothenburg through Incluso. Start is in April, 9-month limited contract to begin with, with possibility of extension after that. This role is 100% onsite in Gothenburg.  

  

Please submit your CV as soon as possible since we will review the applications on an on-going basis.


For more information about this role, please contact Kanika Singhalrecruiter at Incluso.

title: Head of Intelligent Statistics and Analytics (ISA)
emails: fredrik.tengvall@siemens-energy.com, Mikael.nimvall@siemens-energy.com, unionen.finspang.se@siemens-energy.com, asi.se@siemens-energy.com, anders.fors@siemens-energy.com, mikael.malmgren@siemens-energy.com
description:
### **A Snapshot of Your Day**
Join us in a pivotal leadership role where you will spearhead the newly formed Intelligent Statistics and Analytics (ISA) team within the Customer Technical Support (CTS) organization and reporting directly to the RDC Head. Imagine leading a group of talented Analytics Developers and Reliability Engineers, driving innovation through data analytics and reliability engineering. You will define and execute strategies that leverage data-driven insights to enhance product and service reliability across the organization. Collaborate with internal stakeholders to ensure alignment and foster a culture of high performance and accountability. This is your opportunity to make a significant impact on our business objectives and contribute to our vision of excellence.**How You’ll Make an Impact*** Lead, mentor, and manage a high-performing team of Analytics Developers and Reliability Engineers, fostering accountability and professional growth.
* Implement departmental policies and strategies to achieve short-term results for the ISA team, ensuring resource allocation aligns with operational goals.
* Direct the development and implementation of statistical models and analytical tools to enhance product reliability and support services.
* Drive process improvements based on data-driven insights, ensuring the quality and integrity of all analytical outputs and technical solutions.
* Collaborate with peer teams and influence stakeholders to adopt new analytical practices, serving as the primary point of contact for the ISA team’s deliverables.

**What You Bring*** A Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, Data Science, or a related technical field.
* Extensive experience in data analytics, software development, or reliability engineering, with a proven track record in managing technical teams.
* Strong leadership and management skills, with the ability to build and nurture high-performing teams.
* Proficiency in analytical skills, systems engineering, and process improvement methodologies.
* Excellent collaboration and influencing skills, with experience in budgeting and project management.

**About the Team**
The ISA team consists of employees with a variety of backgrounds in engineering, data science, programming and reliability engineering. The ISA team is focused on sustaining & developing analytics for sensor data from the GS D fleet RDC’s and collecting, aggregating and delivering insights from fleet Operation statistics. The team is global, mainly located in the PCC locations for the products such as Finspång in Sweden and Lincoln & Warwick in UK but also in RAP and RLA.
Our **Gas Services division** offers Low-emission power generation through service and decarbonization. Zero or low emission power generation and all gas turbines under one roof, steam turbines and generators. Decarbonization opportunities through service offerings, modernization, and digitalization of the fleet.
We can offer you employment benefits such as: reduction of working hours, advance vacation, health care allowance and an eventual possibility to a flexible working place.**Who is Siemens Energy?**
At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With ~100,000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one sixth of the world's electricity generation.
Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.
Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo**Our Commitment to Diversity**
Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity, we generate power. We run on inclusion and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character – no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.**Application**
Don’t hesitate – apply via https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/en\_US/jobs, id nr 292323 not later than 2026-04-16.
Ongoing selection is applied, the role might be filled before last application date.
For questions about the role, please contact the recruiting manager Fredrik Tengvall on fredrik.tengvall@siemens-energy.com
For questions about the recruitment process please contact the responsible recruiter Mikael Nimvall on Mikael.nimvall@siemens-energy.com
We refrain from all contact with staffing and recruitment companies, or advertising brokers.

title: Control System Engineer
emails: ralf.samuelsson@siemens-energy.com, narcissa.tellefsen@siemens-energy.com, unionen.finspang.se@siemens-energy.com, asi.se@siemens-energy.com, anders.fors@siemens-energy.com, mikael.malmgren@siemens-energy.com
description:
**A Snapshot of Your Day**


Join an open and dynamic workplace where your professional and personal growth is a top priority. As part of our team, you’ll help design control systems for customer gas turbines, tackling varied tasks and enjoying flexible working hours. Whether you’re just starting your engineering career or bring years of experience, you’ll receive a personalized education package and a dedicated mentor to support your journey. Imagine collaborating with colleagues from around the world, developing innovative solutions, and making a real impact from day one.

**How You’ll Make an Impact**

* Collaborate with sub project managers, mechanical, and electrical engineers to adapt turbines, auxiliaries, and control systems to customer requirements
* Lead project adaptation of control programs, design and program new technical solutions, purchase hardware/software, configure networks, and manage customer meetings, documentation, simulation, and testing
* Support workshop testing and commissioning, working through the entire project lifecycle from order start to customer handover
* Engage with a supportive work group within the control system department, sharing knowledge and ideas
* Prepare the technical scope for specific maintenance projects

**What You Bring**

* Engineering degree in Automation, Electrical Engineering, Mechatronics, or similar
* Minimum 3 years’ experience working with control system T3000 and/or PCS7
* Open-minded approach and ability to work professionally with international customers
* Interest in work process improvement is a plus
* Excellent English communication skills; Swedish and other languages are meritorious
* Experience in commissioning, troubleshooting, and networks is beneficial

**About the Team**


You’ll join a diverse team of 15 professionals from around the world, united by a passion for technical excellence and mutual support. The team values collaboration, sharing solutions and ideas to build strong technical knowledge. New members benefit from an internal mentorship program, ensuring a smooth transition and ongoing development.


Our **Gas Services division** offers Low-emission power generation through service and decarbonization. Zero or low emission power generation and all gas turbines under one roof, steam turbines and generators. Decarbonization opportunities through service offerings, modernization, and digitalization of the fleet.


We can offer you employment benefits such as: reduction of working hours, advance vacation, health care allowance and an eventual possibility to a flexible working place.

**Who is Siemens Energy?**


At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With ~100,000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one sixth of the world’s electricity generation.  

Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.  

Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo

**Our Commitment to Diversity**


Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity, we generate power. We run on inclusion, and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character—no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.

**Application**


Don’t hesitate – apply via https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/en\_US/jobs , id nr 292158 not later than 2026-04-16.


Ongoing selection is applied, the role might be filled before last application date.


For questions about the role, please contact the recruiting manager Ralf Samuelsson on ralf.samuelsson@siemens-energy.com


For questions about the recruitment process please contact the responsible recruiter Narcissa Gvozdar Tellefsen on narcissa.tellefsen@siemens-energy.com


We refrain from all contact with staffing and recruitment companies, or advertising brokers.

title: Software Engineers
description:
*MSAB is a global leader in digital forensic technology for mobile device examination and analysis. The MSAB vision is to empower every investigation with digital forensic solutions – for a safer world. A core value is to run an ethics-driven company with innovation and results. The sole focus of MSAB is on delivering high quality digital forensic solutions to collect, review, analyze and manage data. MSAB is a trusted partner to Law Enforcement, Defence Forces, Government Agencies and Forensic Laboratories in democratic countries throughout the world enabling them to investigate crime, gather intelligence, investigate fraud and fight corruption.*
 To our new Stockholm office we’re looking for two engineers to join our Unicore team. You enjoy building systems that are not only functional, but also efficient, scalable, and thoughtfully designed. This is a chance to work with technology with a purpose – to create a safer world.
   

  

**The job**
 At MSAB, the Unicore team is responsible for the core of our digital forensics platform, including UNIFY and the XRY file format (Core API). We’re now working on the next generation of UNIFY, and this is an opportunity to help shape it from the inside.
   

  

 In this role, you will work with core system development where performance and reliability are critical. You’ll be dealing with distributed systems and data-intensive workloads, contributing to architectural decisions with long-term impact, and exploring how to best leverage hardware such as CPU and GPU.
   

  

 Our current tech stack includes C99, C++20, and C# (.NET 8/10), along with gRPC, SQLite and PostgreSQL, running across both Windows and Linux environments.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 We believe you will thrive in this environment if you have a genuine interest in programming and problem-solving, and if you care about performance, clean design, and user experience. You likely enjoy systems engineering, think in terms of trade-offs, and prefer solving meaningful problems over fitting into rigid roles. An appreciation for simplicity and keeping dependencies lean will serve you well.
   

  

**Your Experience**
 You will benefit from your experience with DevOps and CI/CD practices, containerization with Docker, or cloud platforms such as AWS or Azure which is considered a plus.
   

  

 You will be part of a focused team working close to the core of the product, where your decisions matter and your code has real impact.
   

  

 Interested, or know someone who might be? Reach out or apply — we’d love to connect.
   

  

 There is much more to talk about, like values, our growth plans, technology choices etc., but we save that for the interview.
   

  

 Welcome to apply now!
   

  

 For more information about MSAB, please visit: http://www.msab.com
   

  

 What we can offer
   

  

 Create a safer world with us!
   

  

 As one of the world’s leading digital forensics companies, MSAB leads the way in pioneering advancements that contribute to making our world safer.
   

  

 Your work at MSAB will bridge the gap between technology and justice. You will be inspired to go beyond your comfort zone, and you will deal with challenging and meaningful projects. When you join us, you become part of a global team that aspires to create a safer digital future, one breakthrough at a time.
   

  

 We can offer you an exciting work environment, where we value being innovative, acting ethically and delivering results in everything we do while our customers' needs are at the center. We are proud to be a fast-growing international company with more than 20+ nationalities and we work with things that really make a difference.
   

  

 Every day at MSAB is an opportunity to redefine the future of digital forensics.
   

  

 Are you up for the challenge?
   

  

 Department Development Role C++ Developer Locations Stockholm, Sweden Remote status Hybrid

title: Senior Developer till Kleer
description:
**It all comes Kleer!**
 Kleer (tidigare PE Accounting) grundades för att revolutionera ekonomi- och lönebranschen genom ledande teknik och ekonomiexpertis. Vår vision är att vara den obestridda ledaren inom FaaS, den nya standarden för hur företag hanterar ekonomi, lön & tillväxt. Vi söker nu en Senior Developer till vårt tech-team i Stockholm.
   

  

**Rollen Som Senior Developer På Kleer**
 Som Senior Developer hos oss får du en central roll i att utveckla och förbättra vår plattform – både tekniskt och strategiskt. Du tar helhetsansvar för initiativ som påverkar flera delar av systemet, vägleder andra utvecklare och bidrar till att säkerställa hållbar, effektiv och värdeskapande utveckling.
   

  

 Du kombinerar teknisk skicklighet med affärsförståelse och en stark vilja att lära, dela kunskap och samarbeta. Hos oss jobbar du nära produktägare för att skapa lösningar som verkligen gör skillnad för våra kunder och användare.
   

  

 Här får du vara med och forma produktens framtid – i en miljö där vi värderar kvalitet, innovation och kollegialt lärande högt.
   

  

**Dina ansvarsområden**
* Driva tekniska initiativ som spänner över flera komponenter och systemdelar – från idé till leverans.
* Ansvara för teknisk kvalitet, arkitektur, prestanda, säkerhet och hållbarhet i din del av applikationen.
* Bidra i hela teknikstacken – från backend i Java till frontend-lösningar i React.
* Utvärdera och införa ny teknik – alltid med fokus på affärsnytta, skalbarhet och långsiktig hållbarhet.
* Coachning och mentorskap för kollegor i kodkvalitet, design, estimering och tekniska avvägningar.
* Bidra till en kultur av kontinuerligt lärande, konstruktiv feedback och öppen kunskapsdelning.
* Delta i felsökning, incidenthantering och förbättring av våra utvecklingsflöden och observability-lösningar.


**Vår Utvecklingsmiljö**
 Vi har en modern, agil utvecklingsprocess där teamen själva sätter sina milstolpar och prioriteringar. Varje squad ansvarar för en del av applikationen – men jobbar i hela teknikstacken, med fokus på att förstå användarnas behov och skapa verkligt kundvärde.
   

  

 Vår kodbas är till största del skriven i Java, med Spring, Docker, REST, och MySQL
 **,** 
 vårt frontend byggs med react. Miljön är containerbaserad och vi releasar varje vecka. Vi arbetar datadrivet, med automatiserade tester och ett starkt fokus på kvalitet.
   

  

 Vi håller oss alltid uppdaterade med nya verktyg och versioner, och ser
 **AI** 
 som ett naturligt stöd i vår utvecklingsprocess – för att effektivisera, testa och inspirera.
   

  

 Vi står inför en spännande fas där vi flyttar vår plattform till molnet – ett initiativ där vi lär oss tillsammans, delar erfarenheter och bygger framtidens infrastruktur steg för steg.
   

  

**Vem är du?**
* Har flera års erfarenhet av Java och SQL, gärna i kombination med moderna ramverk och verktyg som Spring och Docker.
* Har erfarenhet eller intresse för frontendutveckling i React – eller vill bidra i en fullstackmiljö där backend och frontend möts.
* Erfarenhet av molnplattformar, mikrotjänster eller containerbaserad arkitektur är meriterande.
* Har djup förståelse för systemdesign, arkitektur och hållbar kodkvalitet.
* Trivs i en roll där du både kodar, coachar och tänker långsiktigt.
* Är nyfiken på ny teknik och gillar att utvärdera hur den kan skapa värde.
* Kommunicerar tydligt och bidrar till ett öppet och samarbetsinriktat klimat.
* Talar och skriver svenska obehindrat.


**Var vi finns**
 Vi sitter i ett fint kontor på
 **Fleminggatan 14 i centrala Stockholm** 
 – en plats för både fokus och gemenskap. Här tar vi gärna en pingismatch, samlas runt whiteboarden eller kör en spontan idéworkshop. Samtidigt vet vi att flexibilitet i vardagen är viktigt – därför erbjuder vi
 **hybridarbete** 
 .
   

  

**💛 Om Kleer**
 Kleer grundades 2011 (tidigare under namnet PE Accounting) med visionen att automatisera och förenkla ekonomihanteringen för små och medelstora bolag. Vi har vuxit organiskt, med sunda finanser, och har idag närmare
 **200 kollegor** 
 i
 **Stockholm, Göteborg och Malmö** 
 . Vi är sjufaldiga
 **DI Gaseller** 
 , stolta över vår innovationskraft och våra värderingar – och ännu mer stolta över människorna som gör resan möjlig.
   

  

**Låter det som du?**
 Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!  Vi genomför bakgrundskontroller i slutskedet av våra rekryteringsprocesser.
   

  

**Sökord** 
 : Java, Spring, Docker, REST, Linux, MySQL, Microservices, IntelliJ, Gitlab, Remix, Fullstack, Backend, Produktbolag
   

  

 Kleer är ekonomi- och lönehantering för moderna SaaS- och konsultbolag. Vi kombinerar Sveriges mest automatiserade ekonomisystem med ett dedikerat ekonomiteam. Resultatet: finansiell kontroll i realtid, minimal administration och en ekonomi som driver affären – inte bara rapporterar om den.
   

  

 Vi har utsetts till DI Gasell sju gånger och är stolta över vår värderingsstyrda kultur som bygger på ansvar, glädje, nyfikenhet och prestigelöshet. Idag är vi cirka 200 medarbetare med kontor i centrala Stockholm, Göteborg och Malmö.

title: Senior Fullstackutvecklare
description:
Om jobbet
 


 Vi söker en Fullstackutvecklare som gillar ett Agilt arbetssätt enligt Scrum-modellen. Du kommer att jobba i nära samarbete med kunden och teamet. Arbetet bidrar till att hjälpa människor då stor del av vår verksamhet finns inom e-hälsa och sjukvård.
 




  





****Om rollen****





 Att arbeta som systemutvecklare hos oss innebär bland annat:
 



* Nyutveckling och förvaltningsarbete i interna projekt och för kund
* Ansvar för att release görs i verifiering och produktion
* Presentation av förslag av designlösningar utifrån kravspecifikation
* Fullstackutveckling



  





****Kvalifikationer****



* Kandidatexamen eller högskoleutbildning inom systemutveckling
* Minst 10 års erfarenhet av fullstackutveckling
* Mycket goda kunskaper i:
   



 - Angular
   



 - .NET
   



 - C#
   



 - MSSQL
   



 - PostgresSQL
   



 - CSS/ HTML
* God förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift på svenska



  





****Meriterande kunskaper****



* UX/UI
* React
* Vue
* Javascript/ Typescript
* Azure AD
* Responsiv design
* Kubernetes
* Tillgänglighet WCAG 2.1 AA (Gärna AAA på vissa delar)



  





****Vi erbjuder dig****





 Du erbjuds arbete i ett företag med spännande framtidsutsikter där arbetsmiljön präglas av engagemang och delaktighet.
   



 Vi bygger vår konsultverksamhet på våra värdeord: flexibilitet, leveranssäkerhet och personligt engagemang.
 




  






 Hos oss får du arbeta med frihet under ansvar, flexibel arbetstid, kollektivavtal och bra förmåner. Flera gånger om året anordnar vi interna konferenser med fokus på teambuilding och verksamhetsutveckling. Vi har aktiviteter som skapar trivsel och engagemang.
 




  






 Vi strävar hela tiden mot att ha högt i tak och vill veta vad våra anställda tycker och tänker så att vi kan utvecklas både som arbetsgivare och i leverans mot kund. För att uppmuntra till kompetensutveckling premierar vi våra anställda att utbilda och certifiera sig inom relevanta områden, 4 timmar i månaden på arbetstid.
   

  



 Vi fäster stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper. Det finns stora möjligheter till utveckling inom företaget.
 




****Våra förmåner****



* Flextid
* Sjukvårdsförsäkring, DKV
* Friskvårdsbidrag 5000 kr/år
* Kollektivavtal
* Bonus på företagets resultat
* Rekryteringsbonus
* Kurser och kompetensutveckling



  





****Anställningsvillkor****




****Anställningsform:****

 Tillsvidareanställning (6 mån provanställning)
 




****Omfattning****

 : Heltid
 




****Tillträde:****

 Omgående eller enligt överenskommelse
 




****Löneform:****

 Fast lön
 




****Placering:****

 CentralaSundbyberg, minst 50% på plats.
 




  





*****Om Gisys AB*****





 Gisys hjälper organisationer att effektivisera och säkra hanteringen av digital information genom att utveckla, förvalta och underhålla smarta digitala lösningar. Arbetet utförs av våra it-, verksamhets- och managementkonsulter på plats hos respektive organisation och online.
 




  





****Avdelning****





 Anställd hos oss
 




  





****Distansarbete****





 Hybridarbete

title: Principal Safety Engineer
description:
We are looking for a Principal Safety Engineer to join our Autonomous Driving team in Europe. You will play a critical role in ensuring the safety integrity of our Level 4 automated driving systems, working across the full stack - from system architecture to regulatory approval - to bring safe autonomous mobility to real-world deployment.
   

  

**About Us**
 With over 200 million customers in 50+ countries, Bolt is one of the fastest-growing tech companies in Europe and Africa. And it's all thanks to our people.
   

  

 We believe in creating an inclusive environment where everyone is welcome, regardless of race, colour, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, age, or disability.
   

  

 Our ultimate goal is to make cities for people, not cars, and we need your help to achieve this mission!
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Principal Safety Engineer, you will be the technical authority for safety across our Autonomous Driving System (ADS). You will own the end-to-end safety case - from hazard identification and system design through to regulatory approval - ensuring our systems meet the highest standards of functional safety, SOTIF, and cybersecurity.
   

  

 This is not a compliance-focused role. You will shape how safety is embedded across the organisation, influencing system architecture, engineering practices, and decision-making at every level. You will work closely with engineering, product, and regulatory stakeholders to define what “safe enough” means - and ensure it is rigorously achieved and demonstrated.
   

  

**Main tasks and responsibilities:**
* Own and maintain the system-level safety case for the ADS, covering ISO 26262, ISO 21448 (SOTIF), and ISO/SAE 21434.
* Lead hazard analysis (HARA), ODD definition, and safety architecture across perception, planning, and control systems.
* Define and drive verification and validation strategies, including simulation, scenario-based testing, and real-world validation.
* Establish and govern safety processes, including safety reviews, KPIs, and release approvals to fleet.
* Ensure compliance with regulatory frameworks (e.g. UN WP.29, EU AV legislation) and support type approval readiness.
* Collaborate across teams to embed safety principles into system design, development, and deployment.


 Experience with ML safety, explainability, or perception system robustness is nice to have.
   

  

**About you:**
* Extensive experience (10-12+ years) in automotive, robotics, or aerospace systems engineering, including leadership in safety-critical domains.
* Strong expertise in functional safety (ISO 26262), SOTIF (ISO 21448), and automotive cybersecurity (ISO/SAE 21434).
* Proven track record delivering safety cases and supporting certification or type approval for complex systems.
* Deep understanding of autonomous driving system architecture (perception, sensor fusion, planning, control).
* Experience with hazard analysis (HARA), safety case development (e.g. GSN), and model-based systems engineering.
* Familiarity with tools such as DOORS, Polarion, Codebeamer, or similar requirements and safety tooling.


 Experience is great, but what we really look for is drive, intelligence, and integrity. So even if you don’t tick every box, please consider applying if you feel you’re the kind of person described above!
   

  

**Why you’ll love it here:**
* Play a direct role in shaping the future of mobility.
* Impact millions of customers and partners in 600+ cities across 50+ countries.
* Work in fast-moving autonomous teams with some of the smartest people in the world.
* Accelerate your professional growth with unique career opportunities.
* Get a rewarding salary and stock option package that lets you focus on doing your best work.
* Enjoy the flexibility of working in a hybrid mode with a minimum of 2 days in the office each week to foster strong connections and teamwork.
* Take care of your physical and mental health with our wellness perks.
* Some perks may differ depending on your location and role.

title: Senior IBM Maximo lead developer
description:
**Overview** 




 LTM — a Larsen & Toubro company — is an AI-centric global technology services company and the Business Creativity partner to the world’s largest and most disruptive enterprises. We bring human insights and intelligent systems together to help clients create greater value at the intersection of technology and domain expertise. Our capabilities span integrated operations, transformation, and business AI — enabling new ways of working, new productivity paradigms, and new roads to value. Together with over 87,000 employees across 40 countries and our global network of partners, LTM owns business outcomes for our clients, helping them not just outperform the market, but to Outcreate it.
 



  





**Role -** 
 Senior IBM Maximo lead developer
 



**Type of hiring -** 
**B2B** 




**Location:** 
 Södertälje - Can be done remotely, but agreed days on site
 



**Desired start date:** 
 15- 4-26
 



**Duration:** 
 12 months
 



 We are looking for a
 **senior IBM Maximo lead developer** 
 who can
 **own development and incident management** 
 ,
 **lead a team** 
 ,
 **work closely with business and product owners** 
 , and
 **design integrations** 
 , with strong hands‑on expertise in
 **Maximo (MAS), MBO, MIF, and enterprise integrations** 
 .
 



  





 ·
 **Industrial Maintenance and Shopfloor Services IT Group** 




 The Industrial Maintenance and Shopfloor Services IT group specialises in two primary areas supporting global IT applications for production maintenance and delivering scalable IT hardware solutions to Scanias shopfloor.
 



  





 ·
 **Team Context** 




 The current team includes service managers architects analysts data engineers and developers. Youll be joining the team responsible for industrial maintenance applications where a senior position is needed to oversee development and incident management for the MAXIMO product.
 



  





 ·
 **Typical Responsibilities** 




 Your typical responsibilities may involve:
 



 · Leading the developer team
 



 · Engaging with customers at production units as well as the Product Owner
 



 · Helping the business describe incidents or drafting requirements for suppliers
 



 · Assisting the architect with detailed design choices
 



 · Taking part in development projects
 



  





 ·
 **1 IBM Maximo Domain Knowledge** 




 · Strong experience with IBM Maximo preferably Maximo Application Suite MAS Maximo Manage
 



 · Experience in configuring and customizing applications objects domains and workflows
 



 · Good understanding of the Maximo Business Object MBO Framework
 



 · Experience working with Security Groups roles and data restrictions
 



 · Handson experience with Database Configuration and Object Structures
 



 · Solid experience with the Maximo Integration Framework MIF
 



 · Ability to identify and resolve both functional and technical issues in Maximo
 



  





 ·
 **2 Integrations** 




 · Strong experience with the Maximo Integration Framework MIF
 



 · Experience integrating Maximo with external systems using:
 



 o REST APIs
 



 o SOAP web services
 



 o Messaging and integration platforms
 



 · Experience with Kafka or other eventstreaming platforms is an advantage
 



 · Experience with middleware solutions such as ESB and API gateways
 



  





 ·
 **Key Qualities of a Maximo Lead Developer** 




 · Strong technical knowledge of the MBO Framework and MIF
 



 · Experience designing and delivering enterpriselevel integrations
 



 · Ability to lead guide and mentor development teams
 



 · Strong social and communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with developers architects business stakeholders and vendors
 



 · Structured solutionoriented and comfortable taking technical ownership
 



  





 Skills
 



  





**Mandatory Skills :** 
 IBM Tririga, Maximo Functional, Maximo Technical

title: Senior System Expert
description:
**Do you want to use your deep SAP expertise to keep offshore wind assets running safely, efficiently and predictably?**
 As our
 **Senior System Expert** 
 , you will guide the full maintenance & supply‑chain systems landscape across BA Wind and ensure that thousands of operational decisions every day are supported by reliable, intuitive and future‑proof digital solutions.
   

  

 This is your chance to combine hands‑on system involvement with strategic influence — and to help accelerate Vattenfall’s mission to enable a fossil freedom.
   

  

**Be in your element with BA Wind**
 As part of BA Wind you will play a key role in one of the most exciting pipelines in the industry with 24GW of capacity stretching out more than a decade ahead across our key European markets.
   

  

 You won’t only be involved in developing projects but pioneering cutting-edge innovation, new business models and ways of working with communities and the environment. Working as part of a leading European utility also means you could be helping a growing number of major businesses and brands achieve their own sustainability goals through partnerships.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 You will be the functional and solution owner of our SAP PM/MM environment and the business lead for system improvements across the international Offshore portfolio. You translate business needs into clear system requirements, partner closely with IT, and make sure that what we build truly supports our people in the field.
   

  

**Your Core Responsibilities Include**
* Hold the business mandate for maintenance & supply‑chain system solutions across BA Wind.
* Define and communicate a clear systems vision aligned with operational and long‑term strategy.
* Lead system implementation projects from scoping to go‑live, including business case development and resource alignment.
* Steer the development portfolio together with the Business Demand Manager and senior stakeholders.
* Actively contribute to configuration discussions, testing, training and readiness activities.
* Connect and align stakeholders across SCMS, Operations, Engineering, Finance, D1R and IT.
* Represent Vattenfall in international governance forums such as ILKUG.
* Identify improvement opportunities and help raise systems capability across the organisation.


**Qualifications**
**Your profile**
 You enjoy working closely with system users, data experts and IT partners — and you bring both expertise and curiosity to every step of the process. You like to connect perspectives, bring clarity, support decision‑making and translate complexity into something practical and useful.
   

  

**You Bring**
* A bachelor’s or master’s degree in Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science or similar.
* 8–10 years of relevant experience, including several years in a senior functional or systems‑driven role.
* Deep knowledge of SAP PM/MM, ideally including S/4HANA.
* Experience leading cross‑functional system implementations and/or portfolio governance.
* Strong communication and stakeholder‑alignment skills in an international context.
* Full professional proficiency in English.


**Location:**
 Kolding, Hamburg, Amsterdam, London or Solna
   

  

 Additional Information
   

  

**Our offer**
**Looking for a career with growth?**
 Looking for a role where your superpowers can be leveraged to the maximum and with limitless opportunities for growth?
   

  

 The scale and ambition of our wind, battery and solar business means you can really find your element with us. We’ll also provide you with all the learning and development you need to expand your horizons.
   

  

 We strive to be the best place to work in the industry with highly competitive pay and conditions and an open, supportive culture. A commitment to a healthy work-life balance aims to ensure everyone plays to their strengths, whatever their background and experiences.
   

  

 You will be part of a growing team of 1,700+ colleagues from 50+ nationalities where our positive approach to diversity is reflected in the fact that more than a quarter of staff are women.
   

  

**Information and apply**
 For more information about the position, you are welcome to contact hiring manager Joyce Tibangay Westi, joycetibangay.westi@vattenfall.com. For more information about the recruitment process, you are welcome to contact our recruiter Anne Vriesema via anne.vriesema@vattenfall.com
   

  

 We welcome your application in English no later than 21.4.2026. We kindly request that you do not send applications by any means other than via our website.
   

  

 At Vattenfall we are convinced that diversity contributes to building a more profitable and attractive company and we strive to be a good role model regarding diversity. Vattenfall works actively for all employees to have the same opportunities and rights regardless of gender, ethnicity, age, transgender identity or expression, religion or other belief, disability or sexual orientation. Click here for further information.
   

  

 The security of Vattenfall and its employees is essential. For that reason, a pre-employment screening will be part of your recruitment process. The screening is based on the role you will fulfil within Vattenfall and will be performed by a third party, Validata.
   

  

 Since Vattenfall is a part of the Swedish critical infrastructure, many of our services are security classed. If this position is security classified the final candidates might be subject to a security vetting process, according to Swedish legislation.
   

  

**Company Description**
 Vattenfall is one of the largest energy companies in Europe. We make electricity and heat and supply energy to millions of customers. We do this in Sweden, Germany, the United Kingdom, France, Denmark, Finland and the Netherlands.
   

  

 With around 21,000 employees, we are enabling a future in which we are less dependent on the use of fossil fuels for our energy. We are at the forefront of the transition to a sustainable energy system. To achieve this ambitious goal, we are looking for talented individuals who, in addition to their passion for their own role, also have a strong team spirit and want to contribute to supporting a meaningful company mission.

title: Product Manager
description:
**About Magma Math**
 Magma is one of the world’s fastest-growing and most celebrated EdTech companies, transforming math education through the power of AI. Our platform turns handwritten solutions into digital insights, empowering teachers to focus on creativity and deeper learning — while giving them real-time visibility into student progress.
   

  

 The result? More inclusive, engaging, and effective math classrooms for all learners.
   

  

 But we’re not just building better tools. We’re tackling one of the biggest challenges in global education: math underperformance. With 1 in 5 students globally struggling in math — a subject that forms the backbone of so many career paths — we believe the opportunity to improve outcomes at scale is massive.
   

  

 We’re already the go-to solution in thousands of schools — and we’re just getting started. As we expand rapidly across the U.S. and Europe, we’re building a team as ambitious as our mission: to help millions of students unlock their full potential in math.
   

  

**The Role**
 The Product & Tech team at Magma is divided into three core experiences: Teacher, Student and District. With a growing user base (and plenty of exciting problems to solve), we’re now hiring Product Managers to strengthen both the Teacher Experience team and the District Experience team.
   

  

 In this role, you will work closely with a team of developers, QA and designers to plan, build and launch new features within one of these experiences. You will own a domain end-to-end - from figuring out what problem is worth solving, to shipping a solution, to making sure it actually creates value for students, teachers, districts and the business.‍
   

  

**Who You Are And What You Know**
 To succeed in the role you need to:
   

  

* Be hands-ons and contribute across full product development phase: user research, design, implementation, testing. This is not a pure project manager role where you are only coordinating
* Be comfortable owning both business and tech outcomes
* Be curious and ready to learn a lot of new things


**Your Responsibilities Will Include**
* Leading a team of developers, QAs and designers
* Leading the development and implementation of a domain within Teacher or District Experience
* Coordinating efforts across the product, tech, customer success and revenue teams


**Requirements**
* Hungry to grow within Product Development and track record of ability to go deep/obsess over projects you've run
* 1-2 years experience as product owner/manager, tech or strategy consultant, product designer, software engineer, data science
* Engineering degree (or equivalent) from top tier university
* Startup experience - you know what you are getting yourself into!
* Experience of fast paced and high performance environment


**Nice to have**
* Experience in edtech or education
* Portfolio of products you've built


**What We Offer**
* Build and ship high touch products with positive impact million of users
* Be part of driving change in an industry
* Fantastic colleagues in a fun environment with great opportunities to learn
* Competitive remuneration & benefit package
* Annual company trip
* An opportunity to work on a meaningful mission and have an exceptional impact on education, society and how children learn math across the world ‍


**Start** 
 : As soon as possible
   

  

**Office location** 
 : Gamla Stan, Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

**Our Recruitment Process (all In Office)**
 1st interview with Hiring Manager and Product team member
   

  

 2nd interview (demo) with Hiring Manager and Product team member
   

  

 3rd interview with Founders and Magma team member
   

  

 Note: We practice a 5 day in office policy, and we look forward to working together closely with you
